content:
Hey everyone — it’s me, Ernest the stray, still alive.
 Apparently, there’s been a Meta slave boom lately, and a ton of high-potential characters and squads have been buried under the meta overlords of Catena, Corpo, Castor, Tawil and 24, leaving few chances for others to shine.
So this time, set against the Stellar Chapter and D-zone backdrop, I’m here to re-introduce some off-meta Aequor cult teams that deserve your attention.
To borrow a line from a certain Bilibili creator:
> “Everyone in Aequor is a support.”
Aequor characters are absurdly flexible — one unit can fill multiple roles depending on your setup. Swap out a few Wheels of Destiny and Covenants, and you’ve basically built a new team. This flexibility is the beating heart of Aequor: the Versatility Realm.
This guide aims to give Aequor players more freedom in team building — for both main story clearing and D-zone exploration — and to share my current favorite lineup: the infamous Caro-Aequor boy band.
thread_title: The Seafarer stray’s Aequor Cult ｜Blue blood realm only has one no used (used gpt trans)

content:
# I. Character Breakdown
## Goliath
**Role**: Main DPS / Sub DPS / STR-based sustain support
**Recommended Wheels & Covenants**:
- Main DPS: 【Crimson Pulse】 ⊻ 【April Benediction】
  - 《Blade of Titan》 + 《Power of the Pious》 ⊻ 《Treasured Rarity》 ⊻ 《Kiss of Repose》 ⊻ 《Hymn of the Sovereign》 ⊻ 《Analysis of Death》
- Sub DPS: 【Twisted Twins・Black】 ⊻ 【April Benediction】
  - 《Amidst the Downpour》 + 《Amber-Tinted Death》 ⊻ 《Dream of the Sea》 ⊻ 《Hymn of the Sovereign》
- STR-Sustain Build: 【Twisted Twins・Black】
  - 《Birth of a Soul》 + 《Amidst the Downpour》 ⊻ 《Grace Through Pain》
Goliath is the textbook example of Aequor versatility. He’s got massive AoE scaling, sturdy HP, and efficient STR output. Even when he’s not your main DPS, he can carry weaker frontline cores through early fights — truly the OG road-clearing war god.
In my view, Goliath shines brightest as Main DPS within the Caro-Aequor boy band — where he can act as both the main damage dealer and the tank.
> One key note: 《Kiss of Repose》 only truly works inside that team comp.
Because his exalt generates Blade’s Dominion, which both retains “prepare” and counts as a Strike, it synergizes extremely well with 《Amidst the Downpour》 and other strike-linked Wheels.
Stacking triple Blade’s Dominion lets you unleash the full potential of 《Amber-Tinted Death》 — pure, unfiltered overkill.
> Pro tip: In the Stellar Chapter, Rusted Scalpel’s bonus damage can trigger Preemptive Revenge, helping you gain STR and Tentacle DAM, while also advancing relic growth. Additionally, the emotions of Aurra, talents of Clementine, and Dullesin’s abilities can further amplify the Tentacle-to-STR conversion from exalt.
thread_title: The Seafarer stray’s Aequor Cult ｜Blue blood realm only has one no used (used gpt trans)

content:
## Faros
**Role**: Sub poison / Card-draw support
**Recommended Wheels & Covenants**:
- Poison Sub-DPS: 【Steppenwolf】
  - 《Amidst the Downpour》 + 《Gift of Decay》 ⊻ 《Grace Through Pain》 ⊻ 《Cursed Binding》
- Card-draw Support: 【Twisted Twins・Black】
  - 《Amidst the Downpour》 + 《Grace Through Pain》 ⊻ 《Happy Magic Show》 ⊻ 《Unbearable Freedom》
Faros recently received what can only be described as a mysterious self-centered poison buff — not exactly a meta-breaker, but certainly more interesting than before. Her poison output still lags behind dedicated toxin dealers and can even fall short of Lily running a pure sustain build.
But used properly, Faros shows her true value as an early-phase poison setter. Her skill Deep Currents can accelerate poison setups for Miryam or GDoll, both of whom often struggle in early fights. Meanwhile, Ocean of Elation can roughly be treated as a 2.5× STR-scaled 1:1 damage-to-poison converter.
Some veterans have already pushed Faros to absurd scores — one even hit 72,000 points in Phantasmal Dive with a Faros main DPS run.
In the Stellar Chapter meta, her exclusive relic now also boosts shielding. Combined with faster exalt cycling and her built-in dispel-and-vulnerability utility, she can pivot into a minor sustain support build.
A card-drawing healer who cleanses and adds survivability? Sounds a lot more appealing than before.
> Pro tip: At end-of-turn, Faros’s poison trigger takes priority over GDoll’s and over incoming poison or bleed damage on your own team. If her poison kill resolves first, you’ll cleanly skip your own DoT damage ticks — essentially winning the damage-over-time race by out-timing the system.
thread_title: The Seafarer stray’s Aequor Cult ｜Blue blood realm only has one no used (used gpt trans)

content:
## Caecus
**Role**: Counter DPS / Sustain support
**Recommended Wheels & Covenants**:
- Counter build: 【Paradox】 ⊻ 【Steppenwolf】 ⊻ 【Dream of Medicine】
  - 《Fin of Sorrow》 + 《Sunfall》 ⊻ 《Desire’s Undertow》 ⊻ 《Queen’s Decree》
- Sustain build: 【Cursed Rabbit】 ⊻ 【Book of Purity Revelation】
  - 《Fin of Sorrow》 + 《Birth of a Soul》 ⊻ 《Table of Scriptures》 ⊻ 《Sail of Providence》
  - 《Birth of a Soul》 + 《Grace Through Pain》 ⊻ 《Heart of Silver》
When traditional counter teams were getting obliterated in the Stellar Chapter, Caecus calmly changed careers — from counter bruiser to shield healer hybrid. Even though counter scaling was effectively capped, his massive HP and relic-based healing buffs let him outlast bosses that would flatten most teams.
The Stellar Chapter buffed his survivability rather than his damage, which gave Caecus a second life in other lineups. He can tank, generate sigils, cleanse Weakness and Vulnerable, and remains the single largest source of Tentacle DAM across all Aequor characters.
In the main story or any D-zone run that includes the Aequor Ring, Caecus is the definition of a safe pick — drop him anywhere, and he just works.
> Pro tip: Any shield gained by Caecus triggers his relic’s healing effect, and that healing also scales with his other recovery bonuses. So in D-zone environments where your Wheels of Destiny are restricted, you can experiment with unorthodox sustain builds using 《Grace Through Pain》 or 《Heart of Silver》 — the so-called Aequor heresy heal loops.
thread_title: The Seafarer stray’s Aequor Cult ｜Blue blood realm only has one no used (used gpt trans)

content:
## Sanga
**Role**: Main DPS / Sustain support / Tentacle support
**Recommended Wheels & Covenants**:
- Main DPS: 【Ring of Chamber 36】 ⊻ 【Burial Ground’s Sighs】
  - 《Will Undyielding》 + 《The Last Verse》 ⊻ 《Grace Through Pain》
- Sustain support: 【Cursed Rabbit】 ⊻ 【Paradox】
  - 《Death of Ms. Coral》 + 《Birth of a Soul》 ⊻ 《Memory Spiral》
- Tentacle support: 【Twisted Twins・White】 ⊻ 【Cursed Rabbit】
  - 《Death of Ms. Coral》 + 《Grace Through Pain》 ⊻ 《Birth of a Soul》 ⊻ 《Heart of Silver》 ⊻ 《Lone Feather》
Sanga became one of the victims of the Stellar Chapter.After thriving through multiple D-tide metas, she fell before the current ruleset — losing some main DPS strength but still holding up decently in mid-tier content. Her main issues now: damage ramps too slowly, her survivability build eats too much arith cost (“fat build”),and for pure support roles, there are simply better options.
For newcomers or players with limited Aequor rosters, she’s a reliable choice;for veterans, she’s a project waiting for the right meta to resurrect her. But since we’re diving into heretic builds, let’s talk Tentacle support.Any shield gained by Sanga triggers Realm of Oblivion, granting Tentacle DAM (shield-based Tentacle scaling).
This means any Wheel that consistently generates shields turns Sanga into a stable Tentacle buffer. Her relic also grants card retention, allowing her to keep “Prepare” cards between turns — clearly intended to make her a Tentacle buffer without clogging arith flow (card economy preservation).
> Pro tip: Sanga’s relic only gives card retention to the commands she starts battle with — for example, the mirror orison or Dream of Medicine that generated at start-of-battle triggers. Cards acquired mid-battle (e.g., via Stellar Chapter resonance effects) will not retain automatically.

## Aurita
**Role:** Main DPS / Sub DPS
**Recommended Wheels & Covenants**:
- Main DPS: 【Dream of Medicine】 ⊻ 【Crimson Pulse】
  - 《Power of the Pious》 ⊻ 《Till the Snow Melts》 + 《Dream of the Sea》 ⊻ 《Path Forsaken》 ⊻ 《By Rose Alone》
- Sub DPS: 【Scarlet Embrace】
  - 《Path Forsaken》 + any Wheel featuring death resistance, sigil, or Keyflare synergy
Aurita got a surprise lift in the Stellar Chapter, though about 30% of her new power relies on her exclusive relic. Unfortunately, her mechanics clash with other Aequor characters who like to retain cards, and aside from her unstable card-draw tool, she offers zero real team utility. With fragile base stats and no defensive scaling, she has no choice but to go full DPS — main or sub.
That said, when she’s the DPS, there’s no shortage of units that can carry her. If you have Corposant, or if future updates introduce new discard/aftershock archetypes, Aurita will become a prime sub-DPS candidate. Since nearly every Aequor unit interacts with Tentacle mechanics, her ability to command Tentacles at nearly no cost makes her ascend right alongside them.
As a multi-hit unit who spawns derivative cards, she also supports a heretical combo in the Stellar Chapter: running both 《Wheel Unseen》 and 《Eternal Requiem》. This setup buffs both critical damage and Keyflare generation simultaneously
 (double crit + Keyflare generation build). I can’t test it myself since my +12 Wheels are elsewhere — but the idea lives rent-free in my mind.
> Pro tip: Aurita’s exalt consumes Gland Division cards before drawing new ones, so activating exalt first and then drawing ensures cleaner deck quality — a subtle optimization for advanced Aequor pilots.
thread_title: The Seafarer stray’s Aequor Cult ｜Blue blood realm only has one no used (used gpt trans)

content:
## Murphy
**Role**: Keyflare support / Sustain support / Tentacle support
**Recommended Wheels & Covenants**:
 - Keyflare–Sustain type — 【Life Drain】
  - 《Spring in Acushnet》 + 《Shrouded Birth》 ⊻ 《Merciful Nurturing》 ⊻ 《Moment of Reunion》
 - Keyflare–Tentacle type — 【Twisted Twins・White】
  - 《Shrouded Birth》 + 《Heart of Silver》 ⊻ 《Rewinding Time》 ⊻ 《Slumber Beneath the Glacier》
Murphy, the mother of Aequor, stands alongside Thais and Horla as one of its pillar supports. She didn’t receive any notable buffs in the Stellar Chapter, yet her importance only grows. With the right partners, she can even act as a sub-DPS through creative combos.
In practice, the distinction between her builds lies mostly in resource allocation. If the team’s arithmatica must be centralized on others, Murphy should run the Keyflare–Sustain build: this setup allows her to maintain exalt uptime without relying on Strike or Defence cards.
However, if the team relies on Tentacle output and can spare the resource flow for her, the Keyflare–Tentacle build leverages her shields to amplify Tentacle DAM. Since 《Shrouded Birth》 grants Keyflare whenever she gains shields, combining it with 《Heart of Silver》 forms a self-feeding loop — generate shields, gain Keyflare, cast more Keys, repeat.
> Pro tip: Murphy’s talent converts Sacrifice into Tentacle DAM via round-up calculation. Thus, taking multiple small hits yields more Tentacle DAM gain than a single large one.
> Also, both her relic and the third awakening reduce Sacrifice cost by fixed values. If your focus is minimizing Sacrifice, use “Vortex Reversed” first before Strike or Defence or using Defence/Keyflare commands.
> Conversely, if your goal is to convert Sacrifice into Tentacle gain, Strike or Defence first, then activate “Vortex Reversed”.
thread_title: The Seafarer stray’s Aequor Cult ｜Blue blood realm only has one no used (used gpt trans)

content:
## Tulu
**Role**: Main DPS, Tentacle support
**Recommended Wheels & Covenants**:
- Main DPS — 【Re-evolution】 ⊻ 【Dream of Medicine】
  - 《Hymn of the Sovereign》 + 《Incalculable Factor》 ⊻ 《Aberrant Devour》 ⊻ 《Shepherd’s Staff》 ⊻ 《Blue Stone Throne》
- Tentacle support — 【Re-evolution】 ⊻ 【Twisted Twins・Black】
  - 《Hymn of the Sovereign》 + 《Amidst the Downpour》 ⊻ 《Flamebound Navigator》 ⊻ 《Dream of the Sea》
The green dwarf who crawled out of the sewers—Tulu’s distinction between main DPS and Tentacle support lies in this: the former commands Tentacles himself using Abyss Order and exalt, while the latter amplifies other Tentacle-based characters. In the current Stellar Chapter main story environment, Tulu’s main DPS build hovers near the lower performance limit.
His bulky arithmetic cost (“fat build”) makes rotation clunky compared to the Corpo-Aurita team, and his sustain still falls behind the Caro-Aequor boy band. If the cards line up, he can clear early mobs smoothly; if not, he’ll just have to think really hard and endure.
As for heretic builds, Tulu’s toolkit is absurdly flexible: he increases Tentacle count, improves crit rate, and buffs STR-based Tentacle damage. That allows him to turn almost any multi-hit character into a pseudo-Aurita. We’re talking Rose Aurita, Mouse Aurita, Sixwings Aurita—you name it. Instead of Tulu being the DPS, letting him mass-produce “fake Auritas” tends to be smoother, since the team’s resources don’t all have to sink into his expensive “fat” cards.
> Pro tip: The cost reduction on Abyss Order under the Calm Sea stance isn’t a one-time trigger— as long as Tulu is in Tranquil Sea mode, every copy of Abyss Order (including those created later by Thais, Casiah, Pickma, or Tawil) will cost 2 less arith.
> Under specific conditions, switching into Tranquil Sea to chain more Abyss Orders can achieve solid damage while keeping enough shields to stay alive.
thread_title: The Seafarer stray’s Aequor Cult ｜Blue blood realm only has one no used (used gpt trans)

content:
## Miryam
**Role**: Main DPS, Exalt support, Tentacle support, Sustain support
**Recommended Wheels & Covenants**:
- Strike Main DPS — 【Twisted Twins・Black】 ⊻ 【Steppenwolf】
  - 《Celestial Beast》 + 《Power of the Pious》 ⊻ 《Chains Unbound》 ⊻ 《Hand of Oblivion》
- Poison Main DPS — 【Steppenwolf】 ⊻ 【Dream of Medicine】
  - 《Power of the Pious》 + 《Sunfall》
- Exalt support — 【Burial Ground’s Sighs】 ⊻ 【Organic Form】
  - 《Power of the Pious》 + 《Incalculable Factor》
- Tentacle support — 【Burial Ground’s Sighs】
  - 《Power of the Pious》 + 《Manikin of Oblivion》 ⊻ 《Kiss of Repose》 ⊻ 《Memory Syndrome》
- Sustain support — 【Cursed Rabbit】
  - 《Deathless Ascent》 + 《The Glutton’s Tale》 ⊻ 《Sail of Providence》 ⊻ 《Data is Flesh》
The pioneer who opened the age of “supporting through damage,” Miryam embodies every mechanic imaginable— and thanks to the Ritual Amplification system, she never truly lacks a niche.
Among her many paths, the last two—Tentacle support and Sustain support—are the most approachable. The former enhances her exalt cycle rate, using over-exalt to generate limitless Tentacles beyond normal caps; the latter strengthens Sacrament healing, turning her into a stable healer if you invest in her relic or conviction.
Exalt support builds struggle under current Stellar Chapter rules but still perform decently. After all, Aequor doesn’t have any other direct exalt boosters— she’s the only unit who can literally fill 1.5 bars of exalt for others. However, Miryam’s exalt support requires keeping Obsession in hand, which clashes with Corpo-Aurita’s tempo and clogs deck flow. In contrast, Goliath and Sanga are the most natural partners for exalt-based Miryam.
Her Strike DPS and Poison DPS routes demand high investment, but the payoff is substantial. The former can reach +600% final damage or -100 enemy STR, achievable with a single “Eternal Moment” setup; the latter struggles slightly in early fights but performs well in boss phases. When Strike Miryam uses Exalted Pyre, it’s often enough to reach the poison cap by itself— no need for special Poison optimization.
> Pro tip: The temporary crit damage from Exalted Pyre is fixed, but 《Power of the Pious》 isn’t—you can stack extra crit bonuses before she procs it. While Exalted Pyre boosts STR & Tentacle synergy, Miryam doesn’t really need Tentacle power in the Stellar Chapter— she relies far more on base damage.
>  If your goal is pure poison scaling, trigger a Ritual with another command first, then use Exalted Pyre for best results.
thread_title: The Seafarer stray’s Aequor Cult ｜Blue blood realm only has one no used (used gpt trans)

content:
## Corposant
**Role**: Sustain support, Tentacle support, Main DPS
**Recommended Wheels & Covenants**:
- Sustain build — 【Burial Ground’s Sighs】 ⊻ 【Cursed Rabbit】
  - 《Flamebound Navigator》 + 《The Last Verse》 ⊻ 《Dusk and Down》 ⊻ 《Death of Ms. Coral》
- Tentacle build — 【Burial Ground’s Sighs】 ⊻ 【Dream of Medicine】
  - 《Flamebound Navigator》 + 《Path Forsaken》 ⊻ 《Will Undyielding》 ⊻ 《Happy Magic Show》 ⊻ 《To My Dearest Friend》
- Main DPS — 【Dream of Medicine】
  - 《Blade of Titan》 + 《Power of the Pious》 ⊻ 《Never-Ending Performance》
She’s the woman who brought a sliver of dawn to Aequor— and simultaneously started the civil war between the Retain faction and the Aftershock faction. She revived Aurita and Tulu, then delivered a fatal blow to both Miryam and Tulu in the same breath.
Because the Stellar Chapter converts death resistance into max HP, many fragile teams need death resistance yet have no way to scale it— ending up with neither resistance nor health.
Corposant fixes that. On top of that, she grants STR & Tentacle buffs, grows Tentacles, supports offense, stacks shields, draws cards, and even carries anti-Aequor enemy bonus damage.
If your team already has another Tentacle support—say, in the National Team— then your Sustain Corposant only needs to focus on staying alive. If your setup relies on Corposant as your Tentacle buffer—say, in the Corpo-Aurita team— then her Tentacle build should prioritize exalt cycling and damage efficiency.
But if your investment level is high enough, both Tulu and Aurita are merely Corposant’s wings— she can rise to Main DPS on her own.
> Pro tip: The bonus Lighting Retribution from her relic has no Exhaust and stays in the deck after use. Timing it so she takes damage right before her burst turn to trigger death resistance can dramatically raise her damage ceiling.
> Also, watch your deck’s Pilot count closely—based on experience, when your deck is about to reset, discard remaining Pilot cards at the end of that round. Sacrificing a bit of STR gain ensures the next cycle runs far smoother.
thread_title: The Seafarer stray’s Aequor Cult ｜Blue blood realm only has one no used (used gpt trans)

content:
# II. Special Feature: The Caro-Aequor Boy Band
Advantages:
1. Massive HP pools — the “big-body boys” guarantee max HP scaling.
2. Triple sub-attribute realm mastery ensures Calm Sea and Devour shield efficiency.
3. Three STR-based damage dealers — never worry about low damage output.
4. Two healers — no fear of bleeding out during End of Suffering.
5. Two sigil earners — you’ll never go broke in Ashen Ruins.
6. With just a few Wheel swaps, any member can become the Main DPS to adapt to different bosses or relics.
- And most importantly... they’re all handsome.
Disadvantages:
- High build investment, high gear requirements.
Alternatives:
- Pickma can be swapped with Corposant.
- Caecus can be replaced by Thais or Murphy.
